message:
feat(mux): coalesce PaneOutput notifications and dispatch subscribers outside write lock

Addresses a deadlock hazard and performance bottleneck in mux
notification dispatch (bead wa-7o4f).

Previous behavior: notify() held the subscribers write lock while
invoking every callback via retain(), creating a reentrancy hazard if
any callback tried to unsubscribe or interact with the Mux.  Every
PaneOutput was dispatched individually, causing O(subscribers *
pane_outputs) work per frame — problematic in 50+ pane agent swarms.

New architecture:
- Subscribers stored as Arc<MuxSubscriber> to enable cloning references
  without holding the lock.
- dispatch_notification() takes a read lock, snapshots all subscriber
  Arcs into a Vec, drops the lock, then invokes callbacks.  Dead
  subscribers (returning false) are collected and removed in a second
  pass with a write lock.
- PaneOutput notifications are routed to a new batching path:
  enqueue_pane_output_notification() deduplicates by pane_id using a
  HashSet, then schedules a single flush_pending_pane_output_notifications
  via spawn_into_main_thread.
- flush drains the pending queue and dispatches each coalesced pane_id
  individually, clearing the scheduled flag only when empty.
- Histograms: mux.notifications.subscriber_fanout and
  mux.notifications.pane_output.batch_size.

Tests:
- notification_callbacks_can_unsubscribe_without_lock_reentrancy:
  verifies snapshot-based dispatch lets subscriber A unsubscribe B
  mid-dispatch without deadlock.
- pane_output_notifications_coalesce_until_flushed: verifies
  deduplication, deferred dispatch, and post-flush cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
